title: Profile & Settings
name: Name
ramadanAutoBody: Auto activates on the 1st of Ramadan. Force on to preview the dashboard, off to never switch.
soundHint: Soft chimes when you check off habits, hit streak milestones, claim rewards, finish a book.
angerProtocolHint: Show a one-tap button on Home that opens a 5-step calming protocol for the moment of anger. The exact wording follows your Motivation voice — the Prophetic ﷺ steps when Islamic, a secular calming sequence when Universal.
prayerNote: Times will populate once the prayer-times API is wired up. Your selection is saved.
notifBody1: Mohsen Tracker stays quiet on purpose. Reminders train you to wait for the buzz instead of being the person who shows up — and that's the opposite of what habits are for.
notifBody2: When reminders do ship they'll be off by default, and limited to prayer times, the Iftar countdown during Ramadan, and an opt-in nightly check-in when your streak is on the line.
consequenceBody: How blunt should the "what's at stake" panel be on each habit?
readingHabitBody: Pages logged on books count toward this habit's daily total. Streak and heatmap update automatically.
readingHabitNone: None — keep books separate from habits

consequenceHints.full: All consequences with emphasis. Use if you respond to hard truths.

exportHint: Download every habit, log, streak, reward and punishment in one file.
signOutHint: Wipes the local data on this device. Once Firebase is connected this also signs you out everywhere.
signOutConfirm: This wipes all your habits, streaks, rewards and progress on this device. Continue?
replayTutorialHint: Walk through the same coach-marks you saw on your first visit. Useful after a long break, or to share with someone new on this device.

diagnostics.title: Cloud sync health check
diagnostics.body: If workspaces won't create, cloud sync seems stuck, or you're seeing different data on different devices, run this. It tries each cloud operation and tells you exactly which one is failing — usually because the Firestore security rules in the Firebase console are out of date.
diagnostics.skippedHint: Skipped because an earlier check failed.
diagnostics.guidanceBody: If the workspaceEvent (or any workspace-* row) failed with permission-denied, your Firestore rules in the Firebase console are stale. Copy the full contents of firestore.rules from the repo, paste them into Firebase console → Firestore Database → Rules, click Publish, then run this check again. If a different probe failed, paste the error code here so we can diagnose it.

probes.auth: Signed in with a real account

cloudSync.title: Cloud sync controls
cloudSync.body: Check what's in the cloud vs on this device, force a pull or a push, and confirm both devices are signed in as the same account. If two devices with the same email show different data, it's almost always because they signed in via different methods (Google vs email/password) — compare the uids below.
cloudSync.localStamp: Last cloud write from this device was as (uid)
cloudSync.uidMismatchHint: The uid this device is signed in as does not match the uid that last wrote to the cloud from this device. This usually means you signed out of one account and into another. Pushing now will overwrite the cloud snapshot for your CURRENT account with this device's data; Pulling will replace this device's data with what's in the cloud for the current account.
cloudSync.cloudEmpty: No cloud snapshot for this account yet.
cloudSync.peekError: Couldn't read the cloud snapshot — {code}. Often means stale Firestore rules; run the health check above first.
cloudSync.pullConfirm: Replace this device's data with the cloud version? Anything you've changed here that hasn't synced will be lost.
cloudSync.pullOk: Pulled the cloud snapshot into this device.
cloudSync.pullFail: Pull failed ({result}). Check your connection and try again.
cloudSync.pushConfirm: Overwrite the cloud version with this device's data? Any data on another device that hasn't synced here will be replaced.
cloudSync.pushOk: Pushed this device's data to the cloud.
cloudSync.pushFail: Push failed: {code}. If it says permission-denied, your Firestore rules need updating in the Firebase console.

resetProgressHint: Wipes every habit, log, streak, reward and book on this device and re-runs onboarding. Your sign-in stays — only the local data resets.
resetProgressConfirm: This wipes every habit, streak, reward and progress on this device and starts you over from onboarding. There's no undo. Continue?
deleteAccountHint: Permanently removes your account and any data we hold about you on the server. The local data on this device is wiped too. This can't be undone.
deleteAccountConfirm: This will permanently delete your account, your sign-in, and any cloud-stored progress. There is no recovery. Continue?
deleteAccountReauth: For security, please sign out and sign in again, then retry the delete.
deleteAccountFailed: Couldn't delete the account. Try again, or contact support.
